articles.headline: India's Q1 GDP Growth Exceeds Projections, Signals Robust Recovery
articles.explainer:
• Provisional estimates for Q1 (April-June) 2025-26 show robust GDP growth, surpassing market expectations.
• Manufacturing and services sectors emerge as key drivers of this economic expansion.
• Government attributes growth to strategic policy interventions, capital expenditure push, and improved global demand.
• RBI cautioned about persistent inflationary pressures, suggesting a cautious approach to monetary policy.
• Analysts foresee India maintaining its position as one of the fastest-growing major economies.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Investment models
prelimsPoints: Q1 FY2025-26 (April-June) GDP growth rate.
Key contributing sectors: Manufacturing, Services.
Role of government capital expenditure.
RBI's stance on inflation.
mainsPoints: Analyze factors contributing to India's economic resilience post-pandemic.
Discuss the interplay between fiscal policy (government spending) and monetary policy (RBI's actions) in managing growth and inflation.
Examine the structural reforms needed to sustain high growth rates and create employment.
Evaluate the challenges posed by global economic uncertainties and domestic inflationary pressures.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting India's GDP growth trajectory with ascending bars.
articles.trivia: India's GDP calculation methodologies have evolved significantly since independence, now aligning with international standards set by the UN System of National Accounts (SNA).

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Digital Privacy Rights Amidst Data Sharing Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment reaffirming the fundamental right to privacy in the digital age.
• The ruling came in response to petitions challenging certain provisions of a state-level data sharing policy.
• The court emphasized the need for robust data protection frameworks and consent mechanisms for personal data.
• It reiterated that any state action infringing on privacy must meet the test of legality, necessity, and proportionality.
• The verdict is expected to influence future data governance policies and strengthen individual liberties.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Article 21 of the Indian Constitution and Right to Privacy.
Key aspects of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act).
Role of the Supreme Court in protecting fundamental rights.
Concept of 'Legality, Necessity, and Proportionality' test.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on data governance and state surveillance in India.
Discuss the challenges in balancing national security interests with individual privacy rights in the digital era.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing data protection laws and suggest improvements.
Examine how this ruling impacts the 'Digital India' initiative and citizen-centric services.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a lock and key over digital data, symbolizing data privacy.
articles.trivia: The K.S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the right to privacy a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and Digital Governance

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Focus on Enhanced Maritime Cooperation and Trade
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with leaders reaffirming commitment to deepen strategic partnership.
• Key outcomes included new initiatives for maritime security, sustainable blue economy, and disaster management.
• Discussions centered on enhancing connectivity, both physical and digital, to boost regional trade and investment.
• India reiterated its 'Act East' policy and commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region.
• A joint statement emphasized collaborative efforts against transnational crime and terrorism.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Member countries of ASEAN.
Pillars of India-ASEAN partnership.
Significance of 'Act East' policy.
Key areas of cooperation: maritime, trade, connectivity.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of ASEAN for India's foreign policy and economic interests.
Discuss how enhanced maritime cooperation with ASEAN contributes to India's vision for the Indo-Pacific.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in boosting India-ASEAN trade and investment linkages.
Examine India's role in promoting regional stability and addressing common security concerns with ASEAN.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states displayed together.
articles.trivia: ASEAN was founded on August 8, 1967, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration) by Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand.

articles.headline: New National E-Waste Management Policy 2025 Unveiled, Focus on Circular Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched the National E-Waste Management Policy 2025.
• The policy aims to promote a circular economy model by enhancing collection, recycling, and resource recovery from e-waste.
• It introduces stricter Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) targets and incentives for green design of electronic products.
• Emphasis is placed on formalizing the unorganized e-waste sector and ensuring safe disposal practices.
• Public awareness campaigns and skill development for e-waste handlers are also key components.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Waste management
prelimsPoints: Objectives of the National E-Waste Management Policy 2025.
Concept of Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR).
Difference between formal and informal e-waste recycling sectors.
Impact of e-waste on environment and human health.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges posed by rapidly increasing e-waste generation in India and the inadequacies of previous policies.
Discuss how the new policy promotes a circular economy and its potential benefits for resource conservation and job creation.
Evaluate the role of various stakeholders (producers, consumers, government, recyclers) in successful e-waste management.
Suggest measures to effectively formalize the unorganized e-waste sector and ensure environmentally sound practices.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing electronic waste being processed for recycling.
articles.trivia: India is the third-largest producer of e-waste globally, after China and the United States.

articles.headline: ISRO Achieves Milestone with Successful Test of Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Prototype
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducted a crucial test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating conditions for future orbital re-entry and landing.
• This technology is vital for reducing space launch costs and enhancing India's competitiveness in the global space market.
• The RLV project aims to develop a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) fully reusable launch system.
• The achievement marks a significant step towards India's long-term goal of human spaceflight missions and space tourism.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Purpose of Reusable Launch Vehicles (RLV).
ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) program.
Benefits of reusable launch technology (cost reduction, frequency).
Comparison with other global space agencies' RLV programs.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of developing reusable launch vehicle technology for India's space program.
Discuss how RLVs can contribute to making space access more affordable and sustainable.
Examine the potential economic and geopolitical implications of India's advancements in space technology.
Evaluate the challenges involved in developing and deploying fully reusable launch systems.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle in flight.
articles.trivia: The first experimental flight of ISRO's RLV-TD was successfully conducted on May 23, 2016, from Sriharikota.

articles.headline: RBI MPC Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its current level.
• This decision comes amidst persistent concerns about retail inflation, despite recent moderation in some indicators.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest the RBI is adopting a 'wait and watch' approach, monitoring monsoon impact and global commodity prices.
• The move aims to anchor inflationary expectations and maintain financial stability in the economy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Current Repo Rate and its significance.
Composition and functions of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
Tools of monetary policy (Repo, Reverse Repo, CRR, SLR).
Inflation target set by the government for RBI.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate in the current economic scenario.
Discuss the challenges faced by the MPC in balancing economic growth with inflation control.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in managing supply-side inflation.
Examine the potential impact of global economic factors and domestic variables (e.g., monsoon) on India's inflation outlook.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing the Reserve Bank of India logo with financial charts in the background.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external experts nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: 'Skill India 2.0' Focuses on Green Skills and Future Jobs for Sustainable Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship launched 'Skill India 2.0', an upgraded national skill development mission.
• The new phase prioritizes training in 'green skills' to cater to emerging sectors like renewable energy, sustainable agriculture, and waste management.
• It aims to bridge the skill gap in industries driven by technological advancements and environmental sustainability goals.
• Enhanced digital learning platforms and industry partnerships are central to the mission's implementation.
• The initiative seeks to empower youth with future-ready skills, promoting employment and entrepreneurship in the green economy.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Human Resources
prelimsPoints: Objectives of 'Skill India 2.0'.
Concept of 'green skills' and their importance.
Role of Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship.
Government initiatives for skill development (e.g., PMKVY).
mainsPoints: Analyze the necessity of shifting towards 'green skills' in India's skill development initiatives.
Discuss how 'Skill India 2.0' can address the challenges of unemployment and underemployment among youth.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships in achieving the goals of national skill development missions.
Examine the potential of the green economy to create new job opportunities and contribute to sustainable development.
articles.imageDescription: People learning vocational skills related to solar energy and waste management.
articles.trivia: The original Skill India Mission was launched on July 15, 2015, on World Youth Skills Day.

articles.headline: Enhanced Border Surveillance Along Eastern Frontiers Amidst Regional Instability
articles.explainer:
• Border Security Forces (BSF) and other paramilitary units have significantly ramped up surveillance along India's eastern borders.
• The move comes in response to reports of increased cross-border illegal activities and regional instability.
• Advanced technologies, including drones, thermal imagers, and ground sensors, are being deployed to monitor remote areas.
• Cooperation with neighboring countries on intelligence sharing and coordinated patrols is also being strengthened.
• The objective is to curb illegal infiltration, smuggling, and prevent potential security threats to national integrity.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: Role of Border Security Force (BSF).
Challenges faced in managing India's eastern borders (e.g., terrain, porous nature).
Technological advancements in border surveillance.
Agencies involved in border management.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multifaceted security challenges faced by India along its eastern frontiers.
Discuss the effectiveness of current border management strategies and the role of technology in enhancing security.
Evaluate the importance of diplomatic engagement and intelligence sharing with neighboring countries in addressing cross-border issues.
Suggest comprehensive measures to strengthen border security while ensuring the welfare of border communities.
articles.imageDescription: BSF personnel patrolling a riverine border area in the Northeast.
articles.trivia: The BSF is one of the five Central Armed Police Forces of India, mandated with guarding India's land borders during peacetime and preventing transnational crime.

articles.headline: Delimitation Commission Submits Report for Northeast States, Sparks Political Debate
articles.explainer:
• The Delimitation Commission, constituted for select Northeast states, submitted its final report to the President.
• The report proposes changes in the boundaries of assembly and parliamentary constituencies, along with reservation for ST/SC.
• The exercise was carried out based on the 2001 Census data, as per constitutional provisions.
• While some political parties welcomed the rationalization, others raised concerns over population shifts and representation.
• The recommendations are aimed at ensuring equitable representation and will come into effect after presidential notification.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: Constitutional provisions for delimitation (Articles 82 and 170).
Composition and functions of the Delimitation Commission.
Basis for delimitation (e.g., 2001 Census).
Impact of delimitation on reserved constituencies for ST/SC.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional mandate and objectives of the delimitation exercise in India.
Discuss the challenges faced by the Delimitation Commission in balancing population changes with geographical and administrative considerations.
Evaluate the political and social implications of delimitation in diverse regions like the Northeast.
Examine the arguments for and against freezing delimitation until a later census year.
articles.imageDescription: A map of Northeast India with demarcated electoral constituencies.
articles.trivia: The last nationwide delimitation exercise was completed in 2008, but some states like Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Nagaland, and Jammu & Kashmir had their delimitation deferred or carried out separately.

articles.headline: India Joins Global Alliance for Sustainable Ocean Economy, Boosts Blue Economy Initiatives
articles.explainer:
• India has formally joined the 'Global Alliance for a Sustainable Ocean Economy', a multilateral initiative focused on marine conservation and sustainable resource utilization.
• The alliance aims to foster international cooperation on issues such as ocean pollution, overfishing, and climate change impacts on marine ecosystems.
• India's participation underscores its commitment to the 'Blue Economy' and responsible stewardship of its vast coastline and Exclusive Economic Zone.
• The move is expected to facilitate technology transfer, capacity building, and access to international funding for ocean-related projects.
• This aligns with India's broader strategic interests in the Indo-Pacific region and its environmental commitments.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Environmental pollution and degradation
keyTerms: United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS)
prelimsPoints: Concept of 'Blue Economy'.
Key challenges facing ocean ecosystems globally.
India's coastline and EEZ extent.
International conventions related to ocean governance (e.g., UNCLOS).
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of the 'Blue Economy' for India's economic growth and geopolitical influence.
Discuss how India's participation in global ocean alliances contributes to its environmental commitments and regional leadership.
Evaluate the challenges in achieving sustainable ocean management, including pollution, overfishing, and climate change.
Examine the potential for India to leverage its maritime resources for economic development while ensuring ecological balance.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a pristine coral reef with diverse marine life.
articles.trivia: India's 'Deep Ocean Mission' aims to explore the deep ocean for resources and develop deep-sea technologies.

articles.headline: Western Ghats Biodiversity Under Threat from Climate Change and Anthropogenic Pressures: Study
articles.explainer:
• A new scientific study highlights the escalating threats to the rich biodiversity of the Western Ghats from climate change.
• Rising temperatures, altered rainfall patterns, and extreme weather events are severely impacting endemic species and ecosystems.
• Anthropogenic pressures like habitat fragmentation, deforestation, and unplanned development exacerbate the climate impacts.
• The study calls for urgent, integrated conservation strategies, including corridor preservation and community involvement.
• The Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Geographical extent of Western Ghats.
Status of Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and UNESCO site.
Major threats to its biodiversity (climate change, human activities).
Examples of endemic species in Western Ghats.
mainsPoints: Analyze the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the various threats it faces from climate change and human activities.
Discuss the implications of biodiversity loss in the Western Ghats for regional ecosystems and local communities.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current conservation policies and protected area networks in the region.
Suggest integrated approaches for biodiversity conservation, balancing development needs with ecological preservation.
articles.imageDescription: A lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with dense forests and mist-covered peaks.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats is home to at least 325 globally threatened species, including the Nilgiri Tahr and Lion-tailed Macaque.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Ethical Guidelines for AI in Healthcare, Emphasizing Patient Safety
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog, in collaboration with health and tech experts, released comprehensive ethical guidelines for Artificial Intelligence (AI) in healthcare.
• The guidelines aim to ensure responsible development and deployment of AI tools, prioritizing patient safety, privacy, and data security.
• Key principles include transparency, accountability, fairness, and human oversight in AI-driven diagnostic and treatment systems.
• The policy framework seeks to harness AI's potential to improve healthcare access and outcomes while mitigating risks.
• It recommends a multi-stakeholder approach for continuous evaluation and regulation of AI applications in the medical field.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights
prelimsPoints: Role of NITI Aayog in policy formulation for emerging technologies.
Key ethical considerations for AI in healthcare (e.g., bias, privacy).
Potential benefits of AI in healthcare (diagnosis, drug discovery).
Concept of 'responsible AI'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of Artificial Intelligence in revolutionizing India's healthcare sector.
Discuss the ethical challenges and risks associated with deploying AI in critical areas like patient diagnosis and treatment.
Evaluate the significance of NITI Aayog's guidelines in ensuring a balanced and responsible approach to AI adoption in healthcare.
Suggest regulatory mechanisms and public awareness initiatives to build trust and prevent misuse of AI in medical applications.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of a human brain integrated with circuit board patterns, representing AI.
articles.trivia: NITI Aayog's 'National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence' (2018) identified healthcare, agriculture, education, smart cities, and infrastructure as five priority sectors for AI adoption.

articles.headline: PLI Scheme Expansion Proposed for Advanced Manufacturing Sectors to Boost Domestic Production
articles.explainer:
• The government is considering expanding the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme to include more advanced manufacturing sectors.
• The proposal aims to attract global investment, enhance domestic manufacturing capabilities, and create high-value jobs.
• Focus areas for expansion include specialized chemicals, advanced electronics, and precision engineering components.
• The PLI scheme offers incentives on incremental sales from products manufactured in India to eligible companies.
• This move aligns with the 'Make in India' initiative and efforts to integrate India into global supply chains for critical goods.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
Investment models
prelimsPoints: Objectives of the PLI scheme.
Sectors currently covered under PLI.
Benefits of PLI for domestic manufacturing.
Concept of 'incremental sales'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the efficacy of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme in boosting India's manufacturing sector and attracting FDI.
Discuss the strategic rationale behind expanding the PLI scheme to advanced manufacturing sectors.
Evaluate the challenges in integrating India into global supply chains and becoming a manufacturing hub.
Examine how PLI schemes can contribute to 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' while ensuring global competitiveness.
articles.imageDescription: A factory floor with advanced machinery and workers, symbolizing modern manufacturing.
articles.trivia: The PLI scheme was first introduced in March 2020 for three sectors and has since been expanded to cover 14 key sectors.

articles.headline: Report Highlights Persistent Gender Gap in Digital Literacy in Rural India
articles.explainer:
• A recent report by a leading NGO reveals a significant and persistent gender gap in digital literacy across rural India.
• The study indicates that women in rural areas have considerably lower access to digital devices and internet connectivity compared to men.
• Barriers include socio-cultural norms, lack of affordable access, and limited digital skills training opportunities.
• This digital divide hinders women's access to education, economic opportunities, and participation in governance.
• The report recommends targeted interventions, community-led training, and policy support to bridge this critical gap.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Social empowerment
keyTerms: Pradhan Mantri Gramin Digital Saksharta Abhiyan (PMGDISHA)
prelimsPoints: Definition of 'digital literacy'.
Key findings regarding gender disparity in digital access.
Government schemes aimed at promoting digital literacy (e.g., PMGDISHA).
Socio-economic factors contributing to the digital divide.
mainsPoints: Analyze the socio-economic implications of the persistent gender gap in digital literacy for rural women in India.
Discuss how the digital divide affects women's access to education, healthcare, and economic opportunities.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing government initiatives in promoting digital inclusion and bridging the gender gap.
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including policy, technological, and community-based interventions, to empower rural women digitally.
articles.imageDescription: A rural Indian woman learning to use a smartphone with the help of a trainer.
articles.trivia: The Pradhan Mantri Gramin Digital Saksharta Abhiyan (PMGDISHA) is one of the world's largest digital literacy programs, aiming to make 6 crore rural households digitally literate.

articles.headline: Remembering Quit India Movement: A Call for Self-Reliance and National Unity
articles.explainer:
• As India observes the anniversary of the Quit India Movement (August 8, 1942), its legacy of self-reliance and national unity is revisited.
• Launched by Mahatma Gandhi, the 'Do or Die' call galvanized millions against British rule, marking a crucial phase in the freedom struggle.
• The movement, though suppressed, demonstrated the unwavering resolve of Indians for complete independence.
• Historians emphasize its role in fostering a sense of collective identity and preparing the ground for eventual British withdrawal.
• The principles of non-violence, mass mobilization, and self-determination continue to inspire contemporary national discourse.
gsSubTopics: Indian History - significant events, personalities, issues
The Freedom Struggle - its various stages and important contributors /contributions from different parts of the country
prelimsPoints: Date of the Quit India Movement launch.
Key leaders associated with the movement.
Slogan associated with the movement ('Do or Die').
Causes and immediate effects of the movement.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors that led to the launch of the Quit India Movement and its significance in India's freedom struggle.
Discuss the role of Mahatma Gandhi and other leaders in mobilizing mass support for the 'Do or Die' call.
Evaluate the immediate and long-term impacts of the movement on British colonial rule and the eventual independence of India.
Examine how the ideals of self-reliance and national unity from the movement remain relevant in contemporary India.
articles.imageDescription: A historical photograph of Mahatma Gandhi addressing a crowd during the Quit India Movement.
articles.trivia: The Quit India Resolution was passed on August 8, 1942, at the Bombay session of the All-India Congress Committee.

articles.headline: Lokpal Committee Recommends Measures for Greater Transparency in Public Procurement
articles.explainer:
• A committee constituted by the Lokpal has submitted recommendations to enhance transparency and accountability in public procurement processes.
• The suggestions include mandatory online publication of tender documents, bidder details, and contract awards.
• Emphasis is placed on leveraging technology to minimize human interface and reduce scope for corruption.
• The report also advocates for a robust grievance redressal mechanism and whistleblower protection.
• These measures aim to strengthen good governance, prevent malpractices, and ensure optimal utilization of public funds.
gsSubTopics: Governance, Transparency and Accountability, e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures
Role of Civil Services in a Democracy
prelimsPoints: Mandate and functions of the Lokpal.
Importance of transparency in public procurement.
Role of e-governance platforms like GeM.
Provisions for whistleblower protection.
mainsPoints: Analyze the systemic issues and challenges contributing to corruption and lack of transparency in India's public procurement system.
Discuss how the recommendations of the Lokpal committee can enhance accountability and efficiency in government purchases.
Evaluate the role of technology and e-governance in curbing malpractices and promoting good governance.
Examine the broader impact of transparent public procurement on public trust, economic efficiency, and national development.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of a balance scale with 'transparency' and 'accountability' written on its pans.
articles.trivia: The Lokpal and Lokayuktas Act, 2013, provides for the establishment of a Lokpal for the Union and Lokayuktas for States to inquire into allegations of corruption against public functionaries.

articles.headline: Monsoon Activity Intensifies in Peninsular India, Boosting Reservoir Levels
articles.explainer:
• The Southwest Monsoon has intensified across several parts of Peninsular India, bringing widespread rainfall.
• States like Karnataka, Kerala, and parts of Tamil Nadu have received significant precipitation in the last 24-48 hours.
• This surge in monsoon activity has led to a welcome increase in water levels in major reservoirs crucial for irrigation and drinking water.
• Farmers in rain-fed regions are optimistic about Kharif crop prospects due to improved soil moisture.
• However, weather authorities have issued warnings for potential localized flooding in low-lying areas.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of world’s physical geography

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: Mechanism of Southwest Monsoon in India.
Importance of monsoon for Indian agriculture.
Major rivers and reservoir systems in Peninsular India.
Difference between Kharif and Rabi crops.
articles.imageDescription: Satellite image showing cloud cover over Peninsular India during monsoon.
articles.trivia: The Southwest Monsoon contributes to about 75% of India's annual rainfall.

articles.headline: India-Sri Lanka Hold High-Level Dialogue on Maritime Security and Fisheries Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka conducted a high-level dialogue focusing on enhancing maritime security cooperation and resolving fisheries disputes.
• Discussions included joint patrols, intelligence sharing, and capacity building for maritime surveillance.
• The two nations reiterated their commitment to finding a lasting solution to the long-standing issue of Indian fishermen straying into Sri Lankan waters.
• Both sides emphasized the importance of a secure and stable Indian Ocean region for regional prosperity.
• The dialogue underscored the strong bilateral ties and shared security interests in the strategically vital Indian Ocean.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Security challenges and their management in border areas
prelimsPoints: Geographical proximity of India and Sri Lanka.
Key aspects of India-Sri Lanka relations.
The Palk Strait and its significance.
Issues related to Katchatheevu Island.
mainsPoints: Analyze the complex nature of India-Sri Lanka relations, particularly concerning maritime security and fisheries issues.
Discuss the various dimensions of the fisheries dispute and the challenges in finding a mutually acceptable solution.
Evaluate India's strategic interests in maintaining close ties with Sri Lanka for regional security in the Indian Ocean.
Suggest diplomatic and economic measures that India can adopt to strengthen bilateral cooperation and resolve contentious issues.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships from India and Sri Lanka conducting a joint exercise in the Indian Ocean.
articles.trivia: The Palk Strait, separating India and Sri Lanka, is a rich fishing ground but also a sensitive maritime boundary.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020: A Five-Year Assessment on Implementation Progress
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive five-year assessment report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 was released.
• The report highlights significant progress in curriculum reform, digital education integration, and teacher training.
• Challenges persist in achieving universal foundational literacy and numeracy, and equitable access to higher education.
• Stakeholders emphasize the need for increased public investment and decentralized implementation to accelerate reforms.
• The NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Key pillars of NEP 2020.
Goals related to foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN).
Proposed changes in school and higher education structure.
Role of technology in NEP implementation.
mainsPoints: Critically assess the progress and challenges in implementing the National Education Policy 2020 after five years.
Discuss how NEP 2020 aims to address issues of quality, equity, and access in Indian education.
Evaluate the effectiveness of curriculum reforms and digital initiatives in transforming the learning landscape.
Suggest strategies for overcoming existing bottlenecks and ensuring the successful realization of NEP's vision.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom engaged in collaborative learning activities.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the third major education policy in India's history, following those of 1968 and 1986.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Constructive Dialogue on Indus Waters Treaty Issues
articles.explainer:
• Delegations from India and Pakistan held a constructive dialogue on issues related to the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT).
• The Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) meeting addressed concerns over proposed hydroelectric projects and water sharing mechanisms.
• Both sides reaffirmed their commitment to the IWT, emphasizing its role as a foundational agreement for water cooperation.
• Technical experts exchanged data and discussed potential modifications to project designs to ensure compliance with the treaty.
• The dialogue is seen as a positive step towards de-escalating tensions and fostering cooperation on shared water resources.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: Year of signing of Indus Waters Treaty.
Rivers allocated to India and Pakistan under IWT.
Role of the Permanent Indus Commission (PIC).
Concept of 'run-of-the-river' projects.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the Indus Waters Treaty as a model for water sharing agreements and its role in India-Pakistan relations.
Discuss the persistent challenges and disputes that arise despite the treaty, particularly concerning hydroelectric projects.
Evaluate the importance of continued dialogue and technical cooperation in managing shared water resources between riparian states.
Examine the potential implications of climate change on the Indus Basin and future water security for both nations.
articles.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River system and its tributaries.
articles.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty was brokered by the World Bank and signed by India and Pakistan in 1960.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Initiative for Mangrove Conservation and Coastal Resilience
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched a new national initiative dedicated to mangrove conservation.
• The program aims to restore degraded mangrove ecosystems and enhance their role in coastal protection and biodiversity.
• It emphasizes community participation, scientific research, and sustainable livelihoods for coastal populations.
• Mangroves are critical for buffering coastal communities against extreme weather events and supporting diverse marine life.
• The initiative aligns with India's climate action goals and commitments to biodiversity protection.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Ecological importance of mangroves (e.g., coastal protection, carbon sequestration).
Major mangrove regions in India.
Threats to mangrove ecosystems.
Government initiatives for mangrove conservation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the vital ecological and economic roles played by mangroves in India's coastal regions.
Discuss how mangrove conservation contributes to climate change adaptation and disaster risk reduction for coastal communities.
Evaluate the challenges in effective mangrove restoration and management, including anthropogenic pressures and climate impacts.
Suggest integrated strategies for mangrove conservation that involve local communities and promote sustainable coastal development.
articles.imageDescription: A dense mangrove forest with its intricate root system visible during low tide.
articles.trivia: The Sundarbans, shared by India and Bangladesh, is the largest single block of tidal halophytic mangrove forest in the world and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

highlights: India's Q1 GDP growth surpasses projections, driven by manufacturing and services, signaling robust economic recovery amidst inflation concerns.
Supreme Court reaffirms fundamental right to digital privacy, setting a precedent for data protection and governance frameworks.
India and ASEAN enhance cooperation on maritime security and trade, bolstering 'Act East' policy and Indo-Pacific vision.
National E-Waste Management Policy 2025 unveiled, promoting circular economy and stricter Extended Producer Responsibility.
ISRO successfully tests Reusable Launch Vehicle prototype, marking a significant step towards cost-effective space access and future missions.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV)' program of ISRO, consider the following statements:
1. The RLV aims to reduce space launch costs and enhance India's competitiveness.
2. It is designed as a single-stage-to-orbit (SSTO) system for immediate operational use.
3. The recent test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating orbital re-entry.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: RLVs are developed to significantly reduce launch costs by making components reusable. Statement 2 is incorrect: The RLV project aims to develop a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) fully reusable launch system, not SSTO, and it is in prototype/demonstrator phase for future use. Statement 3 is correct: The test involved a controlled landing experiment, crucial for proving re-entry capabilities.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for keeping the repo rate unchanged amidst inflation concerns in India?
options: (b) Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is mandated to determine the policy interest rates (like the repo rate) to achieve the inflation target while keeping in mind the objective of growth. The other options are not directly responsible for setting the repo rate.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'National E-Waste Management Policy 2025':
1. It primarily focuses on reducing e-waste generation through a ban on electronic imports.
2. The policy promotes a circular economy model by enhancing collection and recycling.
3. It introduces stricter Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) targets.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The policy focuses on a circular economy and responsible management, not primarily on banning imports. Statement 2 is correct: Promoting a circular economy model through enhanced collection, recycling, and resource recovery is a core objective. Statement 3 is correct: The policy aims to introduce stricter EPR targets and incentives for green design.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Act East' policy, often associated with India's engagement with ASEAN, primarily aims to:
options: (a) Enhance India's military presence in East African nations.
(b) Strengthen economic, strategic, and cultural relations with the Indo-Pacific region.
(c) Promote India's cultural heritage in Western European countries.
(d) Establish a free trade agreement exclusively with China and Japan.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Strengthen economic, strategic, and cultural relations with the Indo-Pacific region.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'Act East' policy is a diplomatic initiative by India to promote economic, strategic, and cultural relations with the Indo-Pacific region, particularly Southeast Asian countries, as well as East Asia and Oceania. It evolved from the 'Look East' policy.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following are considered 'Biodiversity Hotspots' in India?
1. Western Ghats
2. Eastern Himalayas
3. Indo-Burma Region
4. Sundaland
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All four regions listed - Western Ghats, Eastern Himalayas, Indo-Burma Region, and Sundaland (which includes Nicobar Islands) - are recognized as biodiversity hotspots in India.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the multifaceted challenges confronting India's economic growth trajectory despite recent positive indicators. Discuss the role of both fiscal and monetary policies in fostering sustainable and inclusive growth.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment. Government Budgeting. Investment models.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's current economic scenario. Detail challenges (inflation, global slowdown, employment, income inequality, structural issues). Explain fiscal policy tools (government spending, taxation) and monetary policy tools (repo rate, CRR) and their impact. Conclude with a balanced approach for sustainable and inclusive growth.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's pronouncements on the right to privacy are crucial in shaping India's digital future. Critically examine the implications of these judicial interventions on data governance, citizen rights, and the 'Digital India' initiative.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure. Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by referencing the Puttaswamy judgment and recent rulings on digital privacy. Discuss how privacy as a fundamental right impacts data collection, sharing, and state surveillance. Analyze its implications for the DPDP Act, 'Digital India' initiatives, and citizen trust. Conclude with challenges and the path forward for balancing innovation with rights.

mainsMocks.question: The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 aims for a holistic transformation of India's education system. Evaluate the progress made in its implementation over the past five years and identify the key bottlenecks that need to be addressed for its successful realization.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce NEP 2020's vision and key pillars (curriculum reform, digital education, FLN, higher education restructuring). Discuss areas of progress (e.g., curriculum framework, ABC, NRF). Identify bottlenecks (funding, teacher training, infrastructure, digital divide, implementation capacity). Conclude with recommendations for overcoming these challenges.

quickBytes.text: The Q1 FY2025-26 GDP growth indicates a robust recovery, with manufacturing and services as key drivers.
The Supreme Court reiterated that any state action infringing on privacy must meet legality, necessity, and proportionality tests.
India's 'Act East' policy is a cornerstone of its engagement with the ASEAN region, focusing on economic and strategic ties.
The new National E-Waste Management Policy 2025 emphasizes a circular economy model and Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR).
ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program aims to reduce space launch costs and enhance indigenous capabilities.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) comprises six members: three from RBI and three external experts.
'Skill India 2.0' prioritizes 'green skills' to prepare youth for jobs in sustainable development sectors.
The Delimitation Commission ensures equitable representation by redrawing constituency boundaries based on census data.
The Western Ghats is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity, facing climate threats.
The Quit India Movement, launched on August 8, 1942, famously gave the call 'Do or Die' for independence.

GS1.headline: Report Highlights Persistent Gender Gap in Digital Literacy in Rural India
GS1.explainer:
• A recent report by a leading NGO reveals a significant and persistent gender gap in digital literacy across rural India.
• The study indicates that women in rural areas have considerably lower access to digital devices and internet connectivity compared to men.
• Barriers include socio-cultural norms, lack of affordable access, and limited digital skills training opportunities.
• This digital divide hinders women's access to education, economic opportunities, and participation in governance.
• The report recommends targeted interventions, community-led training, and policy support to bridge this critical gap.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Social empowerment
keyTerms: Pradhan Mantri Gramin Digital Saksharta Abhiyan (PMGDISHA)
prelimsPoints: Definition of 'digital literacy'.
Key findings regarding gender disparity in digital access.
Government schemes aimed at promoting digital literacy (e.g., PMGDISHA).
Socio-economic factors contributing to the digital divide.
mainsPoints: Analyze the socio-economic implications of the persistent gender gap in digital literacy for rural women in India.
Discuss how the digital divide affects women's access to education, healthcare, and economic opportunities.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing government initiatives in promoting digital inclusion and bridging the gender gap.
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including policy, technological, and community-based interventions, to empower rural women digitally.
GS1.imageDescription: A rural Indian woman learning to use a smartphone with the help of a trainer.
GS1.trivia: The Pradhan Mantri Gramin Digital Saksharta Abhiyan (PMGDISHA) is one of the world's largest digital literacy programs, aiming to make 6 crore rural households digitally literate.

GS1.headline: Remembering Quit India Movement: A Call for Self-Reliance and National Unity
GS1.explainer:
• As India observes the anniversary of the Quit India Movement (August 8, 1942), its legacy of self-reliance and national unity is revisited.
• Launched by Mahatma Gandhi, the 'Do or Die' call galvanized millions against British rule, marking a crucial phase in the freedom struggle.
• The movement, though suppressed, demonstrated the unwavering resolve of Indians for complete independence.
• Historians emphasize its role in fostering a sense of collective identity and preparing the ground for eventual British withdrawal.
• The principles of non-violence, mass mobilization, and self-determination continue to inspire contemporary national discourse.
gsSubTopics: Indian History - significant events, personalities, issues
The Freedom Struggle - its various stages and important contributors /contributions from different parts of the country
prelimsPoints: Date of the Quit India Movement launch.
Key leaders associated with the movement.
Slogan associated with the movement ('Do or Die').
Causes and immediate effects of the movement.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors that led to the launch of the Quit India Movement and its significance in India's freedom struggle.
Discuss the role of Mahatma Gandhi and other leaders in mobilizing mass support for the 'Do or Die' call.
Evaluate the immediate and long-term impacts of the movement on British colonial rule and the eventual independence of India.
Examine how the ideals of self-reliance and national unity from the movement remain relevant in contemporary India.
GS1.imageDescription: A historical photograph of Mahatma Gandhi addressing a crowd during the Quit India Movement.
GS1.trivia: The Quit India Resolution was passed on August 8, 1942, at the Bombay session of the All-India Congress Committee.

GS1.headline: Monsoon Activity Intensifies in Peninsular India, Boosting Reservoir Levels
GS1.explainer:
• The Southwest Monsoon has intensified across several parts of Peninsular India, bringing widespread rainfall.
• States like Karnataka, Kerala, and parts of Tamil Nadu have received significant precipitation in the last 24-48 hours.
• This surge in monsoon activity has led to a welcome increase in water levels in major reservoirs crucial for irrigation and drinking water.
• Farmers in rain-fed regions are optimistic about Kharif crop prospects due to improved soil moisture.
• However, weather authorities have issued warnings for potential localized flooding in low-lying areas.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of world’s physical geography

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: Mechanism of Southwest Monsoon in India.
Importance of monsoon for Indian agriculture.
Major rivers and reservoir systems in Peninsular India.
Difference between Kharif and Rabi crops.
GS1.imageDescription: Satellite image showing cloud cover over Peninsular India during monsoon.
GS1.trivia: The Southwest Monsoon contributes to about 75% of India's annual rainfall.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Digital Privacy Rights Amidst Data Sharing Concerns
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment reaffirming the fundamental right to privacy in the digital age.
• The ruling came in response to petitions challenging certain provisions of a state-level data sharing policy.
• The court emphasized the need for robust data protection frameworks and consent mechanisms for personal data.
• It reiterated that any state action infringing on privacy must meet the test of legality, necessity, and proportionality.
• The verdict is expected to influence future data governance policies and strengthen individual liberties.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: Article 21 of the Indian Constitution and Right to Privacy.
Key aspects of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act).
Role of the Supreme Court in protecting fundamental rights.
Concept of 'Legality, Necessity, and Proportionality' test.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on data governance and state surveillance in India.
Discuss the challenges in balancing national security interests with individual privacy rights in the digital era.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing data protection laws and suggest improvements.
Examine how this ruling impacts the 'Digital India' initiative and citizen-centric services.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration of a lock and key over digital data, symbolizing data privacy.
GS2.trivia: The K.S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the right to privacy a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and Digital Governance

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Focus on Enhanced Maritime Cooperation and Trade
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with leaders reaffirming commitment to deepen strategic partnership.
• Key outcomes included new initiatives for maritime security, sustainable blue economy, and disaster management.
• Discussions centered on enhancing connectivity, both physical and digital, to boost regional trade and investment.
• India reiterated its 'Act East' policy and commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region.
• A joint statement emphasized collaborative efforts against transnational crime and terrorism.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Member countries of ASEAN.
Pillars of India-ASEAN partnership.
Significance of 'Act East' policy.
Key areas of cooperation: maritime, trade, connectivity.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of ASEAN for India's foreign policy and economic interests.
Discuss how enhanced maritime cooperation with ASEAN contributes to India's vision for the Indo-Pacific.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in boosting India-ASEAN trade and investment linkages.
Examine India's role in promoting regional stability and addressing common security concerns with ASEAN.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states displayed together.
GS2.trivia: ASEAN was founded on August 8, 1967, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration) by Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand.

GS2.headline: 'Skill India 2.0' Focuses on Green Skills and Future Jobs for Sustainable Development
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship launched 'Skill India 2.0', an upgraded national skill development mission.
• The new phase prioritizes training in 'green skills' to cater to emerging sectors like renewable energy, sustainable agriculture, and waste management.
• It aims to bridge the skill gap in industries driven by technological advancements and environmental sustainability goals.
• Enhanced digital learning platforms and industry partnerships are central to the mission's implementation.
• The initiative seeks to empower youth with future-ready skills, promoting employment and entrepreneurship in the green economy.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Human Resources
prelimsPoints: Objectives of 'Skill India 2.0'.
Concept of 'green skills' and their importance.
Role of Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship.
Government initiatives for skill development (e.g., PMKVY).
mainsPoints: Analyze the necessity of shifting towards 'green skills' in India's skill development initiatives.
Discuss how 'Skill India 2.0' can address the challenges of unemployment and underemployment among youth.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships in achieving the goals of national skill development missions.
Examine the potential of the green economy to create new job opportunities and contribute to sustainable development.
GS2.imageDescription: People learning vocational skills related to solar energy and waste management.
GS2.trivia: The original Skill India Mission was launched on July 15, 2015, on World Youth Skills Day.

GS2.headline: Delimitation Commission Submits Report for Northeast States, Sparks Political Debate
GS2.explainer:
• The Delimitation Commission, constituted for select Northeast states, submitted its final report to the President.
• The report proposes changes in the boundaries of assembly and parliamentary constituencies, along with reservation for ST/SC.
• The exercise was carried out based on the 2001 Census data, as per constitutional provisions.
• While some political parties welcomed the rationalization, others raised concerns over population shifts and representation.
• The recommendations are aimed at ensuring equitable representation and will come into effect after presidential notification.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: Constitutional provisions for delimitation (Articles 82 and 170).
Composition and functions of the Delimitation Commission.
Basis for delimitation (e.g., 2001 Census).
Impact of delimitation on reserved constituencies for ST/SC.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional mandate and objectives of the delimitation exercise in India.
Discuss the challenges faced by the Delimitation Commission in balancing population changes with geographical and administrative considerations.
Evaluate the political and social implications of delimitation in diverse regions like the Northeast.
Examine the arguments for and against freezing delimitation until a later census year.
GS2.imageDescription: A map of Northeast India with demarcated electoral constituencies.
GS2.trivia: The last nationwide delimitation exercise was completed in 2008, but some states like Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Nagaland, and Jammu & Kashmir had their delimitation deferred or carried out separately.

GS2.headline: India Joins Global Alliance for Sustainable Ocean Economy, Boosts Blue Economy Initiatives
GS2.explainer:
• India has formally joined the 'Global Alliance for a Sustainable Ocean Economy', a multilateral initiative focused on marine conservation and sustainable resource utilization.
• The alliance aims to foster international cooperation on issues such as ocean pollution, overfishing, and climate change impacts on marine ecosystems.
• India's participation underscores its commitment to the 'Blue Economy' and responsible stewardship of its vast coastline and Exclusive Economic Zone.
• The move is expected to facilitate technology transfer, capacity building, and access to international funding for ocean-related projects.
• This aligns with India's broader strategic interests in the Indo-Pacific region and its environmental commitments.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Environmental pollution and degradation
keyTerms: United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS)
prelimsPoints: Concept of 'Blue Economy'.
Key challenges facing ocean ecosystems globally.
India's coastline and EEZ extent.
International conventions related to ocean governance (e.g., UNCLOS).
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of the 'Blue Economy' for India's economic growth and geopolitical influence.
Discuss how India's participation in global ocean alliances contributes to its environmental commitments and regional leadership.
Evaluate the challenges in achieving sustainable ocean management, including pollution, overfishing, and climate change.
Examine the potential for India to leverage its maritime resources for economic development while ensuring ecological balance.
GS2.imageDescription: An aerial view of a pristine coral reef with diverse marine life.
GS2.trivia: India's 'Deep Ocean Mission' aims to explore the deep ocean for resources and develop deep-sea technologies.

GS2.headline: Lokpal Committee Recommends Measures for Greater Transparency in Public Procurement
GS2.explainer:
• A committee constituted by the Lokpal has submitted recommendations to enhance transparency and accountability in public procurement processes.
• The suggestions include mandatory online publication of tender documents, bidder details, and contract awards.
• Emphasis is placed on leveraging technology to minimize human interface and reduce scope for corruption.
• The report also advocates for a robust grievance redressal mechanism and whistleblower protection.
• These measures aim to strengthen good governance, prevent malpractices, and ensure optimal utilization of public funds.
gsSubTopics: Governance, Transparency and Accountability, e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures
Role of Civil Services in a Democracy
prelimsPoints: Mandate and functions of the Lokpal.
Importance of transparency in public procurement.
Role of e-governance platforms like GeM.
Provisions for whistleblower protection.
mainsPoints: Analyze the systemic issues and challenges contributing to corruption and lack of transparency in India's public procurement system.
Discuss how the recommendations of the Lokpal committee can enhance accountability and efficiency in government purchases.
Evaluate the role of technology and e-governance in curbing malpractices and promoting good governance.
Examine the broader impact of transparent public procurement on public trust, economic efficiency, and national development.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized image of a balance scale with 'transparency' and 'accountability' written on its pans.
GS2.trivia: The Lokpal and Lokayuktas Act, 2013, provides for the establishment of a Lokpal for the Union and Lokayuktas for States to inquire into allegations of corruption against public functionaries.

GS2.headline: India-Sri Lanka Hold High-Level Dialogue on Maritime Security and Fisheries Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka conducted a high-level dialogue focusing on enhancing maritime security cooperation and resolving fisheries disputes.
• Discussions included joint patrols, intelligence sharing, and capacity building for maritime surveillance.
• The two nations reiterated their commitment to finding a lasting solution to the long-standing issue of Indian fishermen straying into Sri Lankan waters.
• Both sides emphasized the importance of a secure and stable Indian Ocean region for regional prosperity.
• The dialogue underscored the strong bilateral ties and shared security interests in the strategically vital Indian Ocean.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Security challenges and their management in border areas
prelimsPoints: Geographical proximity of India and Sri Lanka.
Key aspects of India-Sri Lanka relations.
The Palk Strait and its significance.
Issues related to Katchatheevu Island.
mainsPoints: Analyze the complex nature of India-Sri Lanka relations, particularly concerning maritime security and fisheries issues.
Discuss the various dimensions of the fisheries dispute and the challenges in finding a mutually acceptable solution.
Evaluate India's strategic interests in maintaining close ties with Sri Lanka for regional security in the Indian Ocean.
Suggest diplomatic and economic measures that India can adopt to strengthen bilateral cooperation and resolve contentious issues.
GS2.imageDescription: Naval ships from India and Sri Lanka conducting a joint exercise in the Indian Ocean.
GS2.trivia: The Palk Strait, separating India and Sri Lanka, is a rich fishing ground but also a sensitive maritime boundary.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020: A Five-Year Assessment on Implementation Progress
GS2.explainer:
• A comprehensive five-year assessment report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 was released.
• The report highlights significant progress in curriculum reform, digital education integration, and teacher training.
• Challenges persist in achieving universal foundational literacy and numeracy, and equitable access to higher education.
• Stakeholders emphasize the need for increased public investment and decentralized implementation to accelerate reforms.
• The NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Key pillars of NEP 2020.
Goals related to foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN).
Proposed changes in school and higher education structure.
Role of technology in NEP implementation.
mainsPoints: Critically assess the progress and challenges in implementing the National Education Policy 2020 after five years.
Discuss how NEP 2020 aims to address issues of quality, equity, and access in Indian education.
Evaluate the effectiveness of curriculum reforms and digital initiatives in transforming the learning landscape.
Suggest strategies for overcoming existing bottlenecks and ensuring the successful realization of NEP's vision.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom engaged in collaborative learning activities.
GS2.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the third major education policy in India's history, following those of 1968 and 1986.

GS2.headline: India-Pakistan Hold Constructive Dialogue on Indus Waters Treaty Issues
GS2.explainer:
• Delegations from India and Pakistan held a constructive dialogue on issues related to the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT).
• The Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) meeting addressed concerns over proposed hydroelectric projects and water sharing mechanisms.
• Both sides reaffirmed their commitment to the IWT, emphasizing its role as a foundational agreement for water cooperation.
• Technical experts exchanged data and discussed potential modifications to project designs to ensure compliance with the treaty.
• The dialogue is seen as a positive step towards de-escalating tensions and fostering cooperation on shared water resources.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: Year of signing of Indus Waters Treaty.
Rivers allocated to India and Pakistan under IWT.
Role of the Permanent Indus Commission (PIC).
Concept of 'run-of-the-river' projects.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the Indus Waters Treaty as a model for water sharing agreements and its role in India-Pakistan relations.
Discuss the persistent challenges and disputes that arise despite the treaty, particularly concerning hydroelectric projects.
Evaluate the importance of continued dialogue and technical cooperation in managing shared water resources between riparian states.
Examine the potential implications of climate change on the Indus Basin and future water security for both nations.
GS2.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River system and its tributaries.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty was brokered by the World Bank and signed by India and Pakistan in 1960.

GS3.headline: India's Q1 GDP Growth Exceeds Projections, Signals Robust Recovery
GS3.explainer:
• Provisional estimates for Q1 (April-June) 2025-26 show robust GDP growth, surpassing market expectations.
• Manufacturing and services sectors emerge as key drivers of this economic expansion.
• Government attributes growth to strategic policy interventions, capital expenditure push, and improved global demand.
• RBI cautioned about persistent inflationary pressures, suggesting a cautious approach to monetary policy.
• Analysts foresee India maintaining its position as one of the fastest-growing major economies.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Investment models
prelimsPoints: Q1 FY2025-26 (April-June) GDP growth rate.
Key contributing sectors: Manufacturing, Services.
Role of government capital expenditure.
RBI's stance on inflation.
mainsPoints: Analyze factors contributing to India's economic resilience post-pandemic.
Discuss the interplay between fiscal policy (government spending) and monetary policy (RBI's actions) in managing growth and inflation.
Examine the structural reforms needed to sustain high growth rates and create employment.
Evaluate the challenges posed by global economic uncertainties and domestic inflationary pressures.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting India's GDP growth trajectory with ascending bars.
GS3.trivia: India's GDP calculation methodologies have evolved significantly since independence, now aligning with international standards set by the UN System of National Accounts (SNA).

GS3.headline: New National E-Waste Management Policy 2025 Unveiled, Focus on Circular Economy
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched the National E-Waste Management Policy 2025.
• The policy aims to promote a circular economy model by enhancing collection, recycling, and resource recovery from e-waste.
• It introduces stricter Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) targets and incentives for green design of electronic products.
• Emphasis is placed on formalizing the unorganized e-waste sector and ensuring safe disposal practices.
• Public awareness campaigns and skill development for e-waste handlers are also key components.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Waste management
prelimsPoints: Objectives of the National E-Waste Management Policy 2025.
Concept of Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR).
Difference between formal and informal e-waste recycling sectors.
Impact of e-waste on environment and human health.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges posed by rapidly increasing e-waste generation in India and the inadequacies of previous policies.
Discuss how the new policy promotes a circular economy and its potential benefits for resource conservation and job creation.
Evaluate the role of various stakeholders (producers, consumers, government, recyclers) in successful e-waste management.
Suggest measures to effectively formalize the unorganized e-waste sector and ensure environmentally sound practices.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic showing electronic waste being processed for recycling.
GS3.trivia: India is the third-largest producer of e-waste globally, after China and the United States.

GS3.headline: ISRO Achieves Milestone with Successful Test of Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Prototype
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully conducted a crucial test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment, simulating conditions for future orbital re-entry and landing.
• This technology is vital for reducing space launch costs and enhancing India's competitiveness in the global space market.
• The RLV project aims to develop a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) fully reusable launch system.
• The achievement marks a significant step towards India's long-term goal of human spaceflight missions and space tourism.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Purpose of Reusable Launch Vehicles (RLV).
ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) program.
Benefits of reusable launch technology (cost reduction, frequency).
Comparison with other global space agencies' RLV programs.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of developing reusable launch vehicle technology for India's space program.
Discuss how RLVs can contribute to making space access more affordable and sustainable.
Examine the potential economic and geopolitical implications of India's advancements in space technology.
Evaluate the challenges involved in developing and deploying fully reusable launch systems.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle in flight.
GS3.trivia: The first experimental flight of ISRO's RLV-TD was successfully conducted on May 23, 2016, from Sriharikota.

GS3.headline: RBI MPC Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its current level.
• This decision comes amidst persistent concerns about retail inflation, despite recent moderation in some indicators.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts suggest the RBI is adopting a 'wait and watch' approach, monitoring monsoon impact and global commodity prices.
• The move aims to anchor inflationary expectations and maintain financial stability in the economy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Current Repo Rate and its significance.
Composition and functions of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
Tools of monetary policy (Repo, Reverse Repo, CRR, SLR).
Inflation target set by the government for RBI.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate in the current economic scenario.
Discuss the challenges faced by the MPC in balancing economic growth with inflation control.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in managing supply-side inflation.
Examine the potential impact of global economic factors and domestic variables (e.g., monsoon) on India's inflation outlook.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic showing the Reserve Bank of India logo with financial charts in the background.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external experts nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: Enhanced Border Surveillance Along Eastern Frontiers Amidst Regional Instability
GS3.explainer:
• Border Security Forces (BSF) and other paramilitary units have significantly ramped up surveillance along India's eastern borders.
• The move comes in response to reports of increased cross-border illegal activities and regional instability.
• Advanced technologies, including drones, thermal imagers, and ground sensors, are being deployed to monitor remote areas.
• Cooperation with neighboring countries on intelligence sharing and coordinated patrols is also being strengthened.
• The objective is to curb illegal infiltration, smuggling, and prevent potential security threats to national integrity.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: Role of Border Security Force (BSF).
Challenges faced in managing India's eastern borders (e.g., terrain, porous nature).
Technological advancements in border surveillance.
Agencies involved in border management.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multifaceted security challenges faced by India along its eastern frontiers.
Discuss the effectiveness of current border management strategies and the role of technology in enhancing security.
Evaluate the importance of diplomatic engagement and intelligence sharing with neighboring countries in addressing cross-border issues.
Suggest comprehensive measures to strengthen border security while ensuring the welfare of border communities.
GS3.imageDescription: BSF personnel patrolling a riverine border area in the Northeast.
GS3.trivia: The BSF is one of the five Central Armed Police Forces of India, mandated with guarding India's land borders during peacetime and preventing transnational crime.

GS3.headline: Western Ghats Biodiversity Under Threat from Climate Change and Anthropogenic Pressures: Study
GS3.explainer:
• A new scientific study highlights the escalating threats to the rich biodiversity of the Western Ghats from climate change.
• Rising temperatures, altered rainfall patterns, and extreme weather events are severely impacting endemic species and ecosystems.
• Anthropogenic pressures like habitat fragmentation, deforestation, and unplanned development exacerbate the climate impacts.
